what_it_does: A standalone @dsCard preview (group 'Brand') showing the five surface layers — canvas, surface, muted, sunken, dark — as swatch chips with name, hex, and a short usage description.
observations: Comment header `@dsCard group="Brand" name="Surface palette" subtitle="Canvas · surface · muted · sunken · dark" viewport="700x230"`.
Five layers: canvas #FBF7EC (page bg, starred ★), surface #FFFFFF (cards on canvas), muted #F1ECDC (section panels), sunken #E8E2CC (dropzones/placeholders), dark #1F1A12 (menu overlays).
The dark chip carries an ink-dot (overridden to a light #FBF7EC fill) demonstrating inverse content on dark.
Lead text: 'Surfaces — five layers, all warm. No cool greys, no pure white as page background.'
Chips use --border-faint outlines and --r-md radius tokens.
standouts: Rule 'No cool greys, no pure white as page background' — white exists only as a card surface, not the page.
Canvas (#FBF7EC) is starred as the default/base surface; matches the ink 'inverse' value.
conventions: Hardcodes the literal surface hexes inline (expected for a colour-definition card).
notes: A swatch card defining the five warm surface layers and where each belongs (page/card/panel/dropzone/overlay).
questions: Do these map one-to-one onto the --bg-canvas/--bg-muted/--bg-dark tokens used in sibling cards?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata lists canvas/surface/muted/sunken/dark and the body shows exactly those five with usage.
apparent_themes: All-warm surface stack, no cool greys
Depth via layered surfaces (canvas → surface → muted → sunken → dark)
White reserved for cards, never the page background
intention_signals: Aims to define the surface elevation system as a warm, ivory-based stack and enforce the no-pure-white-page rule.
novelty: The explicit warm-only surface doctrine and the named five-layer elevation stack with role per layer.
contribution: Contributes the canonical surface/background layer scale (five warm layers with roles and hexes) that a fused system would keep as its elevation/background reference.
summary_for_embedding: A Brand-group design-system preview card showing the five warm surface layers (canvas, surface, muted, sunken, dark) with hex values and per-layer usage roles.
intention_for_embedding: To define the brand's warm surface/elevation stack and enforce the no-cool-grey, no-pure-white-page rule.
